items.title: ReLU piecewise graph
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  neg["x less than 0"] --> zero["f of x equals 0"]
  pos["x greater than 0"] --> ident["f of x equals x"]
  zero --> out["output a"]
  ident --> out
items.caption: ReLU routes negative inputs to zero and passes positive inputs unchanged—two linear pieces meeting at the origin.

items.title: Dying ReLU feedback loop
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  zneg["pre-activation z always negative"] --> relu0["ReLU output is 0"]
  relu0 --> grad0["gradient w.r.t. weights is 0"]
  grad0 --> frozen["weights stop updating"]
  frozen --> zneg
items.caption: Once a neuron stays in the z ≤ 0 region for all data, zero gradient can trap it permanently—the dying ReLU problem.

items.title: Leaky ReLU vs standard ReLU on negative inputs
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  xneg["negative input x"] --> reluoff["standard ReLU outputs 0"]
  xneg --> leakyslope["Leaky ReLU outputs alpha times x"]
  reluoff --> graddead["gradient 0 for weights"]
  leakyslope --> gradlive["gradient alpha for weights"]
items.caption: Leaky ReLU keeps a small negative slope so weights on inactive-looking neurons can still receive gradient signal.
